innerLoopExecution.conflictResolutionPattern: Every parallel-dispatched pair sharing a file (a1+a5 on server.js/products.js; c2+c3 on settings.js) produced a real merge conflict when the second story rebased after the first merged, on top of the pipeline-state.json bookkeeping conflict that fired on every pair regardless of code overlap. Resolved each time by: (1) taking master's (already-merged) side wholesale for pipeline-state.json and any third-party add/add artefact conflicts, (2) merging the real code conflicts additively (kept both sides' distinct route/tab additions, collapsed duplicated shared logic like CSRF token generation into one call reused by both), (3) re-running BOTH affected stories' own test files before pushing. Full detail in workspace/learnings.md's '2026-07-21' entries.
innerLoopExecution.postMergeBookkeepingGapFound: Discovered near end of session: every one of the 9 merged stories' branch-side pipeline-state.json update (stage=branch-complete, prStatus=merged) was LOST during conflict resolution, because 'take master's side' during each merge meant taking master's still-definition-of-ready state, not the story's own completion state -- exactly the known Epic-Nested Story State Bookkeeping gap (CLAUDE.md cdg.6/B2). Fixed via PR #529 (merged): re-applied stage/prStatus/prNumber/health/verifyStatus/testPlan.passing/acVerified directly on master via `node bin/skills advance`, confirmed 0 new integrity failures. Feature-level stage advanced to branch-complete.

checkpoint.contextAtWrite: Session hit its usage limit mid-inner-loop-execution. 9 of 14 stories in the 2026-07-21-web-ui-experience-redesign feature are merged into master (a1, a2, a3, a5, b1, b2, c1, c2, c3), each independently re-verified via real git/gh state, not just trusted from the dispatching subagent's own report. The 10th ready story, a4 (the final story in Epic A, module-grouped rendering + scale gauge), was dispatched and its dependencies (a1+a2+a3) are all merged, but its own completion status is unknown as of this write -- check its worktree (path in innerLoopExecution.a4 above) first thing on resume. The remaining 4 stories (d1-d4, Admin Impersonation epic) are deliberately held, not forgotten -- they need Hamish King's own named human sign-off (High oversight gate) before they can be dispatched at all; this has been flagged to the user twice already and not yet been given. A genuine post-merge pipeline-state.json bookkeeping gap was found and fixed (PR #529) in the last few minutes of this session -- this checkpoint's own pipeline-state.json values reflect the corrected, accurate state.
checkpoint.resumeInstruction: Read this entire state.json first, then workspace/learnings.md's 2026-07-21 entries (GitHub Actions check-suite gap; parallel-dispatch conflict pattern) for full narrative context -- do not rely on any prior conversation history, assume none carries over into the new account/session. Concrete next steps, in priority order: (1) Check agent worktree at .claude/worktrees/agent-a0bc2952bf2be7b85 (branch feature/a4-module-grouped-rendering-scale-gauge) via `git log`/`git status` to determine if a4 made real progress, is still genuinely running, or was also killed by the session limit -- do NOT trust any stale conversation summary of its status, verify fresh. If it has real uncommitted or committed work, resume/finish it in place rather than restarting from scratch; if it has nothing, re-dispatch it fresh per the same pattern used for the other 9 stories (read all of a4's own artefacts under artefacts/2026-07-21-web-ui-experience-redesign/ first -- story, DoR, test-plan, verification-script, decisions.md -- plus the REAL merged code from a1/a2/a3 in src/web-ui/adapters/modules-adapter.js, src/web-ui/routes/products.js, src/web-ui/modules/product-rollup.js, since those stories' shipped code may differ from the story file's original design assumptions). (2) Once a4 is merged, ALL of Epic A, B, and C (10 stories) will be complete -- confirm this and consider whether a consolidated DoD (Definition of Done) pass across all 10 is warranted before moving on, per CLAUDE.md's short-track DoD reminder (though these are standard-track, not short-track, stories -- DoD still applies at step 8 of the pipeline, not yet done for any of them). (3) D1-D4 remain explicitly blocked on the user's own named sign-off -- do not dispatch them without first getting that sign-off directly (re-ask if it still hasn't been addressed; do not assume silence means approval). (4) If any future PR opened in this repo shows zero CI checks (statusCheckRollup empty, and GET /repos/:owner/:repo/commits/:sha/check-suites confirms no GitHub Actions check suite was even created), do not spend much time on it -- see workspace/learnings.md's dedicated entry on this; try marking the PR ready-for-review + a fresh merge-commit push first, escalate to GitHub Support if that doesn't clear it within a couple of pushes. (5) Retrospective-story.md artefacts for the earlier hotfix PRs (#510, #511, #513, #516, #517) shipped without full short-track chains remain an open, non-blocking item carried forward from before this feature's outer loop began -- still not actioned, still not declined by the user.
